title: Jeedai heresy
content:
{{Top|leg|title=''Jeedai'' heresy}}
{{Quote|Pray that the ''Jeedai'' soon liberate us from those who oppress us with terror and violence!|Heretic chant|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Jeedai'' heresy''' was a heretical movement with the [[True Way]] that ultimately helped end the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].

==History==
{{Quote|Name them. This heresy will die on this moon. If every Shamed One here must perish in glorious sacrifice, it will end here.|Tsavong Lah|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''</ref>}}
The heresy began on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], shortly after the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] had conquered the moon and driven the [[New Jedi Order]] off. [[Anakin Solo]], trying to rescue [[Tahiri Veila]], encountered [[Vua Rapuung]], a former [[Warrior caste|Warrior]] whose ex-lover, [[Mezhan Kwaad]], had made him appear to be a [[Shamed Ones|Shamed One]]. Kwaad was also the one who was holding Tahiri. Anakin and Vua agreed to help each other against their common enemy. When the two finally confronted Kwaad, she admitted her role in Rapuung's shaming, and mortally wounded him, shortly before Tahiri decapitated her. Upon learning that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had redeemed Rapuung, other Shamed Ones began to believe that the Jedi could redeem ''all'' Shamed Ones.

Despite efforts by [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] to suppress both news of the Vua Rapuung incident and the "heresy", the heresy continued to spread throughout [[Yuuzhan Vong empire|Yuuzhan Vong space]] among the Shamed Ones and the lower-class workers and slaves. By the time [[Shimrra Jamaane]], the [[Supreme Overlord]] of the Yuuzhan Vong, arrived on [[Coruscant/Legends#Yuuzhan'tar|Yuuzhan'tar]], the heretics were disorganized, and some groups had contradictory views of Rapuung's redemption. [[High Priest/Legends|High Priest]] [[Jakan]], head of the [[Priest caste]], advocated that the heretics be hunted down and killed, but Shimrra decided that they would just try to give them a warning. Jakan, however, inadvertently defined some of their doctrine, when he said that [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] had no connection to [[Yun-Harla]], the Trickster, and [[Yun-Yammka]], the Slayer. Still, the groups remained contradictory.

That all changed after the [[Battle of Ebaq]], when [[Nom Anor]] was forced to flee Shimrra after the disaster caused the death of Warmaster Tsavong Lah. Anor, realizing that this could be the way that he could dethrone Shimrra, assumed the guise of [[Nom Anor|Yu'shaa]], the Prophet, and began forming their doctrine for them. Yu'shaa said that [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was the remaining exaltations of [[Yun-Yuuzhan]], the Creator God of the Yuuzhan Vong, who had died when he created the Universe. Anor later gained an ally in [[Ngaaluh]], a priestess of Yun-Harla, who had heard about the heresy and believed it to be the Truth. Ngaaluh became Anor's spy in [[Shimrra's Citadel]], bringing false accusations against Anor's rivals in the [[intendant caste]], in the hope that it would remove Shimrra's most competent [[Prefect/Legends|prefects]].

After a few weeks, Shimrra realized her treachery and ordered her to be interrogated, but Ngaaluh committed suicide. Deprived of his spy and with the number of converts dropping, Yu'shaa proclaimed the planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] to be the home of the Jedi and a new home for the Shamed Ones. When Anor learned of Zonama Sekot and visited it, he offered to destroy it for Shimrra, if he would reward him. Anor's sabotage of Zonama Sekot failed. Despite the disappearance of Yu'shaa, the heresy had taken a life of its own. When Zonama Sekot appeared in the skies of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the heretics believed that their redemption was at hand, and rebelled against Shimrra and the [[Elite (Yuuzhan Vong)|Elite]]. The chaos they caused was vital to the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] during the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar]]. After the battle, the Shamed Ones were renamed the [[Extolled]], and finally gained the equality that they had sought, on Zonama Sekot.

==Figures in the heresy's sacred tales==
*[[Shimrra Jamaane]]: The [[Rainbow-Eyed Enemy]] who caused the gods to call forth the ''[[Jeedai]]''.
*[[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]: Silverblade, [[Corran Horn/Legends|The-Slayer-of-Shedao-Shai]], defeated [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Shedao Shai]] in personal combat.
*[[Ganner Rhysode]]: The Ganner, [[Battle of the World-Well|killed]] uncounted numbers of [[Warrior caste|warriors]] at the [[Well of the World Brain]]. Believed to guard the entranceway to the Yuuazhan Vong realm of the dead.
*[[Wurth Skidder]]: Resisted breaking by a [[yammosk]].
*[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]: [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] of the [[New Jedi Order]], associated with [[Yun-Yammka]], slew [[Supreme Overlord]] Shimrra (late addition).
*[[Anakin Solo]]: Redeemer of Vua Rapuung, died in glorious combat against the [[voxyn]] to save [[Solo twins|his crèche-mates]].
*[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]]: Twin of Jaina Solo, converted to the [[True Way]], but rejected it for the Jedi. Associated with [[Yun-Yammka]] and later with [[Yun-Shuno]].
*[[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]: Twin of Jacen Solo, slew [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] in personal combat, associated with [[Yun-Harla]].
*[[Tahiri Veila]]: [[Tahiri Veila|One-who-was-shaped]], associated with [[Yun-Ne'Shel]].
